2605.13801.title: Improving Reproducibility in Evaluation through Multi-Level Annotator Modeling
2605.13801.paper_summary: As generative AI models such as large language models (LLMs) become more pervasive, ensuring the safety, robustness, and overall trustworthiness of these systems is paramount. However, AI is currently facing a reproducibility crisis driven by unreliable evaluations and unrepeatable experimental results. While human raters are often used to assess models for utility and safety, they introduce divergent biases and subjective opinions into their annotations. Overcoming this variance is exceptionally challenging because very little data exists to study how experimental repeatability actually improves as the annotator pool grows. Standard evaluation practices typically rely on a small number of annotations per item (often 3 to 5) and lack the persistent rater identifiers necessary to model individual variance across items. In this work, we introduce a multi-level bootstrapping approach to realistically model annotator behavior. Leveraging datasets with a large number of ratings and persistent rater identifiers, we analyze the tradeoffs between the number of items ($N$) and the number of responses per item ($K$) required to achieve statistical significance.
2605.13801.paper_summary_zh: 隨著生成式 AI 模型如大型語言模型（LLMs）變得越來越普遍，確保這些系統的安全性、穩健性和整體可信度至關重要。<lb/>然而，AI 目前正面臨由於不可靠的評估和無法重複的實驗結果所驅動的可重複性危機。<lb/>雖然人類評估者通常用來評估模型的實用性和安全性，但他們在註釋中引入了不同的偏見和主觀意見。<lb/>克服這種變異性是非常具有挑戰性的，因為目前幾乎沒有數據來研究隨著註釋者池的增長，實驗的可重複性實際上是如何改善的。<lb/>標準評估實踐通常依賴於每個項目少量的註釋（通常是 3 到 5 個），並且缺乏必要的持久評估者識別碼來建模項目之間的個體變異。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們引入了一種多層次自助法來現實地建模註釋者行為。<lb/>利用擁有大量評分和持久評估者識別碼的數據集，我們分析了為了達到統計顯著性所需的項目數量（$N$）和每個項目的回應數量（$K$）之間的權衡。
2605.13801.author: Deepak Pandita et.al.
2605.13801.authors: Deepak Pandita, Flip Korn, Chris Welty, Christopher M. Homan
